title: Press Releases | rogergale
h1: Sir Roger Gale Member of Parliament for Herne Bay and Sandwich (including West Thanet)
text_excerpt: --> Press Releases | rogergale top of page Sir Roger Gale Member of Parliament for Herne Bay and Sandwich (including West Thanet) Home Roger's Views Press Releases Current Issues Westminster Offices & Memberships Archive Archive 2025 Archive 2024 Archive 2023 Archive 2022 Archive 2021 Archive 2020 Archive 2019 Archive 2018 Archive 2017 Archive 2016 Archive 2015 Archive 2014 Archive 2013 Archive 2012 Archive 2011 Archive 2010 Archive 2009 Archive 2008 Archive 2007 Gallery Contact Us More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Press Releases 2026 Roger regularly issues press releases about current topics. We have included a selection of them here which we hope will be of interest to you. 18th April 2026 Great turnout for the Save Our Fields “Walk and Talk,” with a John Deere tractor from Thanet farmers Pippa and James Southorn, and a strong case from Pippa for growing our own crops. Then on to Westgate Town Hall for quiz prizes, well done to Kerry Boyd (1st) and Vanessa Crow Brown (2nd), before heading to Dog Acre, Birchington, to judge the village scarecrow competition highlighting the threat to local farmland. The “Traditional Scarecrow” by Birchington in Bloom was a standout. Finally, a quick visit to Pegwell Bay to catch up with talented young filmmaker Connor Carruthers, supporting the Save Minster Marshes campaign against the proposed converter station. If we want to protect our environment, we must stand up and fight for it. 17th April 2026 Yesterday morning I took the opportunity at Business Questions to raise the issue of the failure of the company responsible, Capita, to pay, to retired civil servants, their pensions in a timely and efficient manner. This failure is causing real personal and financial distress to loyal servants of this Country and has to be resolved as a matter of urgency. ￼ The Leader of the House, as always courteous, has agreed to pursue this matter with the Cabinet Secretary. ​ ​ Watch here​ 16th April 2026 The Prime Minister appears to have developed a habit of deflecting difficult questions by claiming that matters “didn’t cross his desk” an approach that raises serious doubts about how decisions are being made at the highest level of government. It is time for him to stop shifting responsibility onto others, accept accountability, and do the honourable thing by resigning. 16th April 2026 On Saturday 18th April WESTGATE & GARLINGE action group will be taking part in the “National Day of Action for nature - to save green space from development”. Their Day of Action event starts at 10am at Dent de Lion Gatehouse with a Walk-'n'-Talk across the farmland footpath ending with a Farmer talk with a tractor at Minster Road (approximately 10.30am). There will also have an Environmental Information Hub at Westgate Town Council Offices will be open between 10-12noon. This is one of the many events happening across Thanet with Birchington having a Scarecrow Competition ( Thanet Scarecrows ) and Save Minster Marshes will be having an event by The Viking Ship in Cliffsend. ​ ​ Watch here​ 16th April 2026 A huge privilege to meet Elliott with his Mother and younger sister at a military families gathering in the House yesterday morning at the start of the month of the Military Child organised by the Royal British Legion. Elliott’s father has served for much of his twenty five years in the army overseas and Elliott’s moving poem reflects his experience as a small boy growing up with a frequently absent dad. Dad has just retired from the armed forces to join Lancashire police force and so at last will at least be based at home. Do we need to be reminded of the sacrifices made not just by our serving men and women but also by their families? Yes. I think that we do. 14th April 2026 Before the Easter recess the Health Secretary, Mr Streeting, used a live interview on Sky television to take a swipe at the maternity unit at the QEQM hospital i
